topic: Claude Opus 4.6 Review 2026: The New SWE-Bench Leader for Coding

competitors.title: Claude Opus 4.6 Deep Dive: Benchmarks, Agent Teams, and the Writing Controversy
key_points: SWE-bench Verified: 80.8% (virtually tied with Opus 4.5 at 80.9%)
ARC-AGI-2: 68.8% - 83% jump from Opus 4.5's 37.6%, biggest reasoning leap ever
Agent Teams: Multi-agent Claude Code orchestration built a working C compiler (100K lines) that boots Linux on 3 CPU architectures
Writing controversy: Users report Opus 4.6 produces flatter, more generic prose vs Opus 4.5
1M context window in public beta, doubles output tokens to 128K
Context compaction enables effectively infinite conversations
Pricing: $5 input/$25 output per million tokens (<=200K), $10/$37.50 for 1M context beta
500+ zero-day vulnerabilities found during pre-release testing

competitors.title: Claude Benchmarks (2026): Every Score for Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6 & Haiku
key_points: Comprehensive benchmark comparison tables across all major tests
SWE-bench Verified: 80.8% Opus 4.6, 79.6% Sonnet 4.6, 73.3% Haiku 4.5
Terminal-Bench 2.0: 65.4% Opus 4.6 vs 77.3% GPT-5.3 Codex
GPQA Diamond: 91.3% Opus 4.6 highest score recorded
Pricing: Opus 4.6 $15/$75 per million tokens, Sonnet $3/$15, Haiku $0.80/$4
1M token context for Opus and Sonnet, 200K for Haiku
Released February 2026, data verified March 5, 2026

competitors.title: Claude Opus 4.6 Review: Minor Update or Major Improvement? Benchmarks & Analysis
key_points: Modest improvements: SWE-bench +1.2% (82.1% vs 80.9%), GPQA +1.6% (66.9% vs 65.3%)
Response time improved by 9.4% (2.9s vs 3.2s for 1000 tokens)
Edge case handling improved from 95% to 98% in coding tests
Complex reasoning accuracy improved from 87% to 92%
Same pricing as Opus 4.5: $15/$75 per million tokens
Comparison: vs GPT-5.2 - Opus maintains quality lead but slower; vs Gemini 3 Pro - Opus better quality but smaller context
Rating 8.5/10, recommendation: upgrade for all production applications
